grid_dimensions_as_printed.note: Same 1/2/3 x E/D/C/B/A grid as the ground-floor plan (page53) and the structural grid master -- x_chain and the E-D/D-C/C-B/B-A segments (2.70/2.30/3.50/1.50) are identical. The only difference is the top overhang dimension (1.10 here vs 2.00 on the ground-floor plan) -- this is the roof-eave overhang ('แนวหลังคา' roofline reference shown dashed on this sheet), a per-floor architectural value, not a grid-line discrepancy.

warnings: LEVEL LABEL CROSS-CHECK CONFIRMED: every room on this upper-floor plan is at +3.75 (or +3.65 for wet areas/balcony) -- this directly confirms that the riser diagram on page52 (SN-04), which printed 'ระดับพื้นชั้นล่าง +3.75' (labeling +3.75 as if it were the ground floor), is very likely a mislabeled/boilerplate-reused level tag on that sheet: +3.75 is actually the upper floor ('ชั้นบน'), matching this house's own SN-06 plan. This is the same finding pattern already documented for house #3's equivalent riser diagram (page49) via its own upper-floor plan (page51). Flagged for human review; not corrected on the riser diagram file itself per extraction rules (transcribe literally, flag discrepancy).

Stair-step numbering is a continuation from the ground-floor plan's steps (which ended at step 6, labeled 'ขึ้น'): this sheet shows steps continuing as roughly 7 through 17 with a mid-landing around 12, labeled 'ขึ้น' again at the top of this flight -- consistent with a switchback (dog-leg) staircase connecting the two floors. Exact per-step numbering hard to fully verify pixel-by-pixel at this resolution; overall sequence transcribed as a range (6/7-11, landing 12, 13-17), not a guaranteed exact digit-by-digit read.

The two small bathrooms (ห้องน้ำ 2, ห้องน้ำ 3) contain fixture icon clusters that are visually present but not individually identifiable with confidence (WC vs LAV vs floor drain) at this image resolution -- flagged for zoom-review rather than guessed.

The small square symbol on the roofline above ห้องน้ำ 3 is ambiguous (possible AVC vent-stack roof penetration, consistent with the vent pipes seen running to roof level on the riser diagram) but not labeled with text at this position -- flagged for zoom-review.

Grid confirmed identical to the ground-floor plan (page53) and the structural grid master; only the top overhang dimension differs (1.10 vs 2.00), which is an architectural roofline value, not a grid discrepancy.
